<p></p><p><em>The "real questions" you need to be asking are laid out here. Don't rush through this. This is your career.</em></p><p><br></p><h1><strong>#1. Let's Unearth Your Story</strong></h1><p>No artist exists in the minds of their fans without a story attached. </p><p>Your fans like you FOR A REASON.</p><p>Beyond the art, I mean. A reason that has to do with THEM.</p><p>They're connecting with something.</p><p>What are they connecting with? Be clear about it. Do you think Taylor Swift can answer this question? Do you think The Weeknd knows the answer? They know in explicit detail.</p><p><strong>WRITE YOUR ANSWER DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>What do you WANT them to connect with?</p><p><strong>WRITE THAT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>Have you been communicating about those aspects with them lately?</p><p>Or only posting selfies?</p><p>And only posting #hashtag filled captions with lame one-liners?</p><p>Even if you don't have a message yet...</p><p>I know you have a story.</p><p>What is it? What about your upbringing, your journey, your process, your beliefs etc... that makes you one-of-a-kind.</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>See how, when you try to write it down, it's a little challenging because you need to figure out what to focus on?</p><p>See how you have to find an angle?</p><p>That's what PR people get paid to do.</p><p>Without an "angle," people won't connect with you.</p><p>That's what makes us human.</p><p>You connect with that girl you met on the plane because of her story.</p><p>And because you have matching scores on 'The Pattern' app.</p><p>You connect that guy at work because of his story.</p><p>And you like the way he sees the world.</p><p>So why aren't you providing this same connection with your fans?</p><p>Now, if you have a message...</p><p>Then wow, do you have it made.</p><p>Your message IS your story.</p><p>Look no further.</p><p>When you have a message...People will feel like they connect with you even if they don't like your art. Crazy right? They connect with the reason why you make your art: Your message.</p><p>All great artists have a message. But you know that already. <a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/12754553" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">That's why you did 'The Drill Down Method' with your mom. </a>So I won't focus on that here. </p><p>If you haven't found your message yet, all is not lost.</p><p>Figure out your story.</p><p>Imagine we're going to share your art with the world. And The New York Times wants to do a story about you or your competition, but they can only choose one. They're going to go with the most compelling story. </p><p>What's YOUR story?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p><em>Alternate version for people who are brand new:</em></p><p><em>If you don't have a story yet, then write what you want that story to be this time next year.</em></p><p><br></p><h1><strong>2. Who are "your people"? Part I: Demographics.</strong></h1><p>Let's start with the basics.</p><p>Think to yourself... What type of person is going to resonate with your story?</p><p>Can you see what this person looks like in your mind?</p><p>Knowing your audience puts your art into better focus. It informs you on how you should communicate with them.</p><p>You may have a message (or, at the very least, a story)... But to know how to communicate it, you have to know what language your audience is speaking.</p><p>Are they feminists? Trump supporters? Athletes? Mothers? Or did they just join the military? </p><p>Who are YOUR fans?</p><p>Let's start with the basics: Demographics.</p><p>Be binary with this exercise. </p><p>For example, is your ultimate fan a boy or a girl?</p><p>Don't say both. Pick one. Even though yes, I know, I know... Both COULD be your fans.</p><p><em>But where is it leaning? </em></p><p>Male or female?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>How old are they?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>Where do they live? A particular country? A particular town?</p><p>Hip hop ended up taking over the world. But it began as a form of expression exclusive to the black community in New York City.</p><p><strong>WRITE DOWN WHERE THEY'RE FROM.</strong></p><p><br></p><p><em>Don't be surprised if when you start describing your audience, that they seem suspiciously a lot like YOU (or you at some point in your life).The only real difference with you and them is 1. your amazing talent, and 2. you were the one who had to guts to pursue your goals. They see their own potential in all your wins.</em></p><h1><strong>3. Who are "your people"? Part II: Psychographics.</strong></h1><p>Now... Think of your ultimate fan.</p><p>The type of person who would be the head of your fan club.</p><p>What part of your personal story would resonate with them the most?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>What do they like most about your art?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>Are they introverts or extroverts?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>What causes do they believe in?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>How do they dress?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>What can't they stop talking about all day? Their relationship. Politics? Tiktok? The environment?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>What do they spend most of their free time doing?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>What kind of music do they like? Classic hip-hop? None?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>What kind of art do they support?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>What "influencers" and public figures do they love?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>Where do they hang out in the real world?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>Where do they hang out online?</p><p><strong>WRITE IT DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><h1><strong>4. Your Avatar</strong></h1><p>Pull all this information together to paint a precise picture of your ultimate fan.</p><p><strong>WRITE THE SYNOPSIS DOWN. </strong></p><p><br></p><p>Is there someone (or a few people) you know in real life close to this picture you've painted?</p><p><strong>WRITE THEIR NAMES DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>If you don't know someone-- Make up a name for your Ultimate Fan.</p><p>This is what we call an "avatar".</p><p>I call them my "muse".</p><p>This is the person that you choose to create art for.</p><p>From now on...</p><p>When you communicate, you will imagine that you're speaking DIRECTLY to them.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>5. Communicate Your Story Everywhere -- Always Directed To Your Fans. Forget The Masses From Now On.</strong></h1><p>The 3 Rules To Communicate Like Someone Who Has A Career, Not A Hobby:</p><h2>1. Relate everything to your story / message.</h2><h2>2. Talk about only things that you have in common with your fans. (You should feel like you know them intimately now.)</h2><h2>3. Never communicate in generalities like you're talking to "the masses". The masses don't matter anymore.</h2><p>If what you want to post doesn't follow these 3 rules, use a "personal" account to post that other bullshit. It doesn't fit in here.</p><p><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/9033828" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Communicate this concise way in EMAILS</a>. </p><p><a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/14606196" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">Talk to "your people" this way in your CAPTIONS</a>. </p><p>Do the same thing in Instagram Stories.</p><p>Do the same in tweets.</p><p>Do it in interviews.</p><p>Communicate this way even when you meet strangers. </p><p>That's right:</p><p>Until you're a household name... Paint an unmistakable picture of who you are for everyone you meet.</p><p>Make it easy for your fans to identify you. Talk their language, and no one else's.</p><p>Guess what? Some people won't like it.</p><p>That's the point!</p><p>THOSE ARE NOT YOUR FANS.</p><p>But for your real fans, everything you do will be a dog whistle calling to their soul.</p><p>===</p><p><br></p><p>Ok, you can have the rest of the day off. Today was huge!</p><p>Tomorrow, you will re-write this into a concise one page document.</p><p>Then you will print it out where you can see it at a glance.</p><p>Every time you get stuck writing an email, or a caption, or when people are asking you to describe what your do...</p><p>Keep coming back to this document to focus you. Do that until it's memorized. And audience-awareness is second nature.</p><p>When you start applying what you've learned about yourself and your fans today...</p><p>You will see an immediate change in how people relate to your work.</p><p>Congratulations on doing the work that most people don't do (and that's why they stand still).</p><p>You have elevated your <a href="https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/23909722" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">creativity / work ethic</a> score by 2000%.</p><p><br></p><h1><strong>6. Help! How do I find my fans?</strong></h1><p>You find them online, the way you would in the tactile world...</p><p>You go where they hang out.</p><p>Now is the time to use the answers to the questions above to do some detective work (it shouldn't be that hard if your fans are a lot like you anyways).</p><p>Do they hang out on Instagram? Well, maybe it's time to start using Instagram better then. Be more strategic about your posts now. Are you inviting people that leave complimentary comments to your mailing list? Do you have a mailing list? Are you using it?</p><p>Use your social media to tell your story. Don't just post your work. Find your unique way to make people discover the artist behind the work.</p><p>How many gatekeepers are you talking to every week?</p><p>Gatekeepers are the people who have access to thousands of people at a time. Blog writers. Photographers. Videographers. These are all people who can put you on to their scene in seconds if they like what you do.</p><p>Have you made a list of 10 gatekeepers that you can reach out to this week to share your story and your work with?</p><p><br></p><p>Who are the bloggers that would fall in love with what you do?</p><p><strong>WRITE THEIR NAMES DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>Who are the "influencers" who would be down with your shit?</p><p><strong>WRITE THEIR NAMES DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>Who are your peers who are steps ahead of you that would love to help you rise?</p><p><strong>WRITE THEIR NAMES DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><p>Who are the business people like managers, agents, publishers, owners that you should be talking to?</p><p><strong>WRITE THEIR NAMES DOWN. (OR WRITE DOWN THE NAMES OF PEOPLE WHO CAN CONNECT YOU TO THEM)</strong></p><p><br></p><p>Who has a podcast that would love to chat with someone that has your type of story?</p><p><strong>WRITE THEIR NAMES DOWN.</strong></p><p><br></p><h1>This master list is gold. Now it's time to make a plan of action to reach X amount of the names on your list every single week for the next 3 months. And follow through with your emails and DMs. Be persistent, but friendly. This is your career. You're world building. Even someone like Drake is doing the same thing. He's just doing it at a higher level. Fuck making more music, or creating another design, or making another video until you've elevated the art you've just made to it's next level. Creating a new piece of art is the bare minimum starting point of having a career. The next step is letting people know you're here... It's time to SHARE the work with as many people as possible. Beyond a few IG story posts on release week. I know your self doubts, and questioning your self worth will be kicking in right about now. Quieten that voice by reaching out to the people on your master list every day. Watch what happens. Your art is worth that effort, no? -- (<em>Your answer will be what you decide to do once you're done reading this.)</em>
</h1><p><br></p><p></p>
</div>
